sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports struggling with depression over the past year due to remote work and social isolation. He describes intermittent numbing sensations in his legs, primarily when sitting for extended periods, and reports that these symptoms improve with movement. He denies tingling or pain in his legs. He reports no weakness in his legs. He states he is able to perform activities of daily living, though he finds prolonged standing and sitting uncomfortable. He is currently taking 40 milligrams of Prozac daily and reports taking it consistently.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals a systolic ejection murmur at 3/6, lungs are clear to auscultation, abdomen is soft and non-tender, lower extremity strength is intact, and there is no edema. The ECG shows stable findings compared to the previous year. The lumbar spine x-ray and back x-ray are stable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary concern is depression, exacerbated by recent stressors. He also reports chronic lower back pain, likely contributing to his overall discomfort. The murmur is stable and does not suggest acute cardiac distress.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a physical therapy referral to address the chronic lower back pain. Increase Prozac dosage to 40 milligrams daily. Order an echocardiogram for follow-up. Order aspirin 81 milligrams daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess response to treatment and monitor symptoms.
